questions.question: Which States currently have a Legislative Council (Vidhan Parishad)?
options: Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Bihar, Uttar Pradesh
Only 4 states — UP, Bihar, Maharashtra, Karnataka
questions.explanation: Currently 6 States have Vidhan Parishad: Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Bihar, and Uttar Pradesh. Tamil Nadu abolished its Vidhan Parishad in 1986.

questions.question: Which party ruled Tamil Nadu for the longest period after 1967?
questions.explanation: Since 1967, Tamil Nadu has been ruled alternately by DMK (founded by M. Karunanidhi) and AIADMK (founded by M.G. Ramachandran). Congress has not won independently since 1967. No other party has formed a government.

questions.question: Periyar E.V. Ramasamy founded which organization?
questions.explanation: Periyar founded the Self-Respect Movement in 1925 and the Dravidar Kazhagam (DK) in 1944. His disciple C.N. Annadurai broke away in 1949 to form the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK).

questions.question: Which Article of the Constitution of India deals with the appointment of the Governor of a state?
questions.explanation: Article 155 states that the Governor of a state shall be appointed by the President by warrant under his hand and seal.

questions.question: How many members are there in the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly consists of 234 members elected from single-member constituencies.

questions.question: Who was the first Chief Minister of Madras State (later Tamil Nadu) after India's independence?
questions.explanation: O. P. Ramaswamy Reddiar became the first Chief Minister of Madras State in 1947.

questions.question: The three-tier Panchayati Raj system in Tamil Nadu is based on the recommendations of which committee?
questions.explanation: The Balwant Rai Mehta Committee (1957) recommended the establishment of a three-tier Panchayati Raj system, adopted by Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: Under which Article can the Governor of Tamil Nadu reserve a state bill for the consideration of the President?
questions.explanation: Article 200 empowers the Governor to reserve a bill for the President's consideration.

questions.question: How many seats in the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly are reserved for Scheduled Castes?
questions.explanation: As per the latest delimitation, 44 seats are reserved for Scheduled Castes in the Tamil Nadu Assembly.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of a State Public Service Commission?
questions.explanation: Article 315 provides for the establishment of a Public Service Commission for the state.

questions.question: The 'Amma Unavagam' scheme of the Tamil Nadu government primarily aims to provide:
questions.explanation: Amma Unavagam (canteens) provide subsidized nutritious meals at low cost to the public.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of the Panchayati Raj system in Tamil Nadu?
options: Appointment of members by the Governor
questions.explanation: Panchayati Raj members are elected, not appointed by the Governor; the Governor does not appoint them.

questions.question: The Preamble of the Indian Constitution declares India to be a:
questions.explanation: The Preamble, as amended by the 42nd Amendment, describes India as a Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, Democratic Republic.

questions.question: Which term was added to the Preamble of the Indian Constitution by the 42nd Amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) added 'Socialist', 'Secular', and 'Integrity' to the Preamble.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution abolishes untouchability?
questions.explanation: Article 17 abolishes untouchability and forbids its practice in any form.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle is based on the Gandhian principle?
questions.explanation: Article 48 (prohibition of cow slaughter) is a Gandhian directive principle.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act is related to which level of government?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) deals with Panchayati Raj institutions in rural areas.

questions.question: Who conducts elections to the Panchayats in Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: The State Election Commission is responsible for conducting Panchayat elections in Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: The High Court of Tamil Nadu is located in which city?
questions.explanation: The principal seat of the Madras High Court is in Chennai, with a bench in Madurai.

questions.question: Which scheme provides free breakfast to school children in Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: The Chief Minister's Breakfast Scheme was launched in 2022 to provide free breakfast to government school students.

questions.question: The Constitution of India is described as?
questions.explanation: India is a quasi-federal state with a strong unitary bias, as per K.C. Wheare.

questions.question: Right to Freedom of Religion is guaranteed under which Articles?
questions.explanation: Articles 25 to 28 of the Constitution guarantee the Right to Freedom of Religion.

questions.question: Which tier of Panchayati Raj is optional for states with a population below 20 lakhs?
questions.explanation: The intermediate level (Block Panchayat) is not mandatory for states with less than 20 lakh population.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of the Indian Constitution borrowed from the Government of India Act, 1935?
questions.explanation: The federal scheme, including the office of Governor and the judiciary structure, was largely borrowed from the Government of India Act, 1935.

questions.question: The concept of 'Justice, social, economic and political' in the Preamble of the Indian Constitution is taken from which revolution?
questions.explanation: The ideals of social, economic and political justice were inspired by the Russian Revolution (1917).

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution are Fundamental Rights guaranteed?
questions.explanation: Fundamental Rights are enshrined in Part III of the Constitution, covering Articles 12 to 35.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles is based on Gandhian philosophy?
questions.explanation: Promotion of cottage industries on a cooperative basis (Article 43) reflects Gandhian economic thought.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992 added which part to the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment added Part IX (Panchayats) to the Constitution, containing Articles 243 to 243-O.

questions.question: Who is the ex-officio Chairman of the Tamil Nadu State Planning Commission?
questions.explanation: The Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu is the ex-officio Chairman of the State Planning Commission.

questions.question: The Election Commission of India is a body consisting of how many members?
options: One Chief Election Commissioner and two Election Commissioners
One Chief Election Commissioner and one Election Commissioner
questions.explanation: Since 1993, the Election Commission has been a multi-member body with one CEC and two ECs.

questions.question: The High Court of Madras was established in which year?
questions.explanation: The Madras High Court was established on 15 August 1862, making it one of the oldest High Courts in India.

questions.question: Which Tamil Nadu government scheme provides free breakfast to primary school students?
questions.explanation: The Chief Minister's Breakfast Scheme was launched in 2022 to provide free breakfast to students in government primary schools.

questions.question: The Governor of a state is appointed by whom?
questions.explanation: The Governor of a state is appointed by the President of India under Article 155 of the Constitution.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution deals with the establishment of Panchayats?
questions.explanation: Article 243 of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of Panchayats at the village, intermediate, and district levels.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Directive Principle of State Policy?
questions.explanation: Directive Principles, like the Right to Work (Article 41), are non-justiciable guidelines for the state, unlike Fundamental Rights which are justiciable.

questions.question: The State Election Commission in Tamil Nadu is responsible for:
options: Conducting elections to the State Legislature
Conducting elections to Panchayats and Municipalities
questions.explanation: The State Election Commission conducts elections to local bodies (Panchayats and Municipalities) in Tamil Nadu, as per Article 243K.

questions.question: The minimum age for voting in India is:
questions.explanation: The 61st Constitutional Amendment Act (1988) reduced the voting age from 21 to 18 years.

questions.question: The Governor of a state is appointed by the:
options: Chief Justice of the High Court
questions.explanation: The Governor is the constitutional head of a state and is appointed by the President of India under Article 155.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Fundamental Duty under the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Fundamental Duties (Article 51A) include 'to safeguard public property and to abjure violence', added by the 42nd Amendment.

questions.question: The highest judicial authority in a state is the:
questions.explanation: The High Court is the highest judicial body in a state, with appellate jurisdiction over lower courts.

questions.question: The concept of 'Panchayati Raj' in India was constitutionalised through which amendment?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act (1992) gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj institutions.

questions.question: The Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu is appointed by the:
options: Chief Justice of Madras High Court
questions.explanation: The Governor appoints the Chief Minister, who is the leader of the majority party in the state legislative assembly.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of Panchayati Raj institutions?
questions.explanation: Article 243 of the Indian Constitution deals with the definition and establishment of Panchayats.

questions.question: In which year was the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act enacted?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act was enacted in 1994 to implement the 73rd Constitutional Amendment.

questions.question: Who appoints the Governor of Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: The Governor of a state is appointed by the President of India under Article 155 of the Constitution.

questions.question: What is the tenure of a member of the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly?
questions.explanation: The normal term of a State Legislative Assembly is five years from the date of its first meeting.

questions.question: Which of the following Fundamental Rights under the Indian Constitution is available only to citizens of India?
options: Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression
questions.explanation: Article 19 (Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression) is available only to citizens, not to foreigners.

questions.question: Which of the following is a flagship social welfare scheme of the Tamil Nadu government?
questions.explanation: Amma Unavagam is a subsidized meal scheme launched by the Tamil Nadu government in 2013.

questions.question: In Tamil Nadu, the local self-government at the urban level is called:
questions.explanation: Urban local self-government in Tamil Nadu is governed by municipalities and municipal corporations.

questions.question: The Election Commission of India is responsible for conducting elections to which of the following in Tamil Nadu?
options: Both Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assembly
questions.explanation: The Election Commission conducts elections to the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies, including in Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: Which Schedule of the Indian Constitution deals with the allocation of seats in the Rajya Sabha for states including Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: The Fourth Schedule allocates seats in the Rajya Sabha to each state and union territory.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution deals with the composition of the Legislative Assembly of a state?
questions.explanation: Article 170 specifies the composition of state legislative assemblies.

questions.question: What is the total number of seats in the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly as per the Constitution?
questions.explanation: After the 2008 delimitation, Tamil Nadu Assembly has 234 seats.

questions.question: The Governor of Tamil Nadu is appointed by whom?
questions.explanation: The Governor is the constitutional head of a state appointed by the President.

questions.question: Which fundamental right prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex or place of birth?
questions.explanation: Article 15 prohibits discrimination on these specific grounds.

questions.question: The Directive Principle aiming to secure a uniform civil code is found in which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 44 directs the state to secure a Uniform Civil Code for citizens.

questions.question: The Panchayati Raj system in Tamil Nadu is based on which constitutional amendment?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) constitutionalized Panchayati Raj institutions.

questions.question: Who conducts elections for local bodies in Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: The State Election Commission is responsible for conducting local body elections.

questions.question: The Madras High Court is located in which city?
questions.explanation: The Madras High Court is situated in Chennai, Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: Which Tamil Nadu scheme provides free breakfast to school children?
questions.explanation: Launched in 2023, the Chief Minister's Breakfast Scheme provides free breakfast to government school students.

questions.question: By which constitutional amendment were the words 'Socialist' and 'Secular' added to the Preamble?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) inserted 'Socialist' and 'Secular' into the Preamble.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct chronological order of the three-tier Panchayati Raj system established in Tamil Nadu?
options: Village Panchayat, Panchayat Union, District Panchayat
Panchayat Union, Village Panchayat, District Panchayat
District Panchayat, Panchayat Union, Village Panchayat
Village Panchayat, District Panchayat, Panchayat Union
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj system follows the standard three-tier structure: Village Panchayat at the base, Panchayat Union (Block) in the middle, and District Panchayat at the apex.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of a State Legislature for Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: Article 168 provides for the constitution of Legislatures in States, including Tamil Nadu, with a Governor and one or two Houses.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act, which governs the local self-government in rural areas, was enacted in which year?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act was enacted in 1994 to implement the 73rd Constitutional Amendment.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Directive Principle of State Policy specifically implemented by the Tamil Nadu government through its midday meal scheme?
questions.explanation: Article 45 directs the State to provide early childhood care and education, which the Tamil Nadu midday meal scheme supports.

questions.question: The Governor of Tamil Nadu appoints the Chief Minister. Under which Article of the Constitution is this power exercised?
questions.explanation: Article 164 states that the Chief Minister shall be appointed by the Governor and other ministers on the advice of the Chief Minister.

questions.question: In the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly, what is the maximum strength of members as per the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 170 allows a maximum of 500 and minimum of 60 members; Tamil Nadu has 234 members.

questions.question: Which Tamil Nadu government policy is directly linked to the Fundamental Right to Education under Article 21A?
questions.explanation: Samacheer Kalvi ensures equal access to education, aligning with Article 21A's right to free and compulsory education.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu State Election Commission is responsible for conducting elections to which bodies?
questions.explanation: Under Article 243K, the State Election Commission supervises elections to Panchayats and Municipalities.

questions.question: Which of the following is a unique feature of the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj system compared to other states?
options: Direct election of Gram Panchayat presidents
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu uniquely provides for the direct election of Gram Panchayat presidents, unlike many other states.

questions.question: The Preamble of the Indian Constitution declares India to be a 'Sovereign Socialist Secular Democratic Republic'. Which word was added by the 42nd Amendment and is reflected in Tamil Nadu's secular policies?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment added 'Secular' to the Preamble, aligning with Tamil Nadu's long-standing secular governance.

questions.question: Which High Court has jurisdiction over the state of Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: The Madras High Court, established in 1862, has jurisdiction over Tamil Nadu and Puducherry.

questions.question: Which of the following parts of the Indian Constitution deals with the Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.explanation: Part IV (Articles 36-51) contains the Directive Principles of State Policy, which are non-justiciable but fundamental in governance.

questions.question: The system of Panchayati Raj in India was constitutionalized through which amendment?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Constitution Amendment Act, 1992 added Part IX to the Constitution, providing a uniform structure for Panchayati Raj institutions.

questions.question: In the Indian Constitution, which Article guarantees the Right to Equality before law?
questions.explanation: Article 14 ensures equality before the law and equal protection of the laws within the territory of India.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of the Preamble to the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Preamble declares India as a Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, Democratic Republic. 'Federal' is not explicitly mentioned in the Preamble.

questions.question: The Governor of a state in India is appointed by whom?
questions.explanation: As per Article 155, the Governor of a state is appointed by the President of India and holds office during the pleasure of the President.

questions.question: Which Tamil Nadu government scheme provides free bus travel for women in the state?
options: Free Bus Pass Scheme for Women
questions.explanation: The 'Free Bus Pass Scheme for Women' (launched in 2021) allows women to travel for free on government buses within Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: Under the Indian Constitution, which body is responsible for the conduct of elections to the Parliament and State Legislatures?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India (Article 324) is an autonomous constitutional authority responsible for conducting free and fair elections.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of the Supreme Court?
questions.explanation: Article 124 establishes the Supreme Court of India, consisting of a Chief Justice and other judges appointed by the President.

questions.question: The concept of 'Basic Structure' of the Constitution was propounded by the Supreme Court in which landmark case?
questions.explanation: In the Kesavananda Bharati case (1973), the Supreme Court held that the basic structure of the Constitution cannot be amended by Parliament.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of the Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.explanation: Directive Principles are non-justiciable (cannot be enforced by courts) but are fundamental to the governance of the country (Article 37).

questions.question: Which Article of the Constitution of India deals with the establishment of the State Finance Commission for local bodies?
questions.explanation: Article 243-I provides for the constitution of a State Finance Commission every five years to review the financial position of Panchayats.

questions.question: The concept of 'District Planning Committee' in Tamil Nadu is derived from which part of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Part IX-A of the Constitution (Article 243-ZD) mandates the constitution of a District Planning Committee to consolidate plans prepared by Panchayats and Municipalities.

questions.question: In the context of Tamil Nadu, the reservation for women in Panchayati Raj institutions is:
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu provides 50% reservation for women in Panchayati Raj institutions, exceeding the constitutional minimum of one-third.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu State Election Commission is responsible for conducting elections to which of the following?
questions.explanation: The State Election Commission is a constitutional body (Article 243K) that supervises elections to local bodies (Panchayats and Municipalities).

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj system?
options: Direct election of Gram Panchayat members
Appointment of District Collector as Chairperson of District Panchayat
Reservation for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes
questions.explanation: The District Panchayat Chairperson is elected indirectly by the members, not appointed by the District Collector.

questions.question: The 'Right to Information Act' in Tamil Nadu was enacted in the year:
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu enacted its own Right to Information Act in 1997, before the central RTI Act of 2005.

questions.question: Under the Tamil Nadu Prohibition of Forcible Conversion of Religion Act, conversion by which method is prohibited?
questions.explanation: The Act prohibits conversion by force, fraud, or allurement, and requires prior notice to the District Magistrate for any conversion.

questions.question: The 'Tamil Nadu Public Service Commission' derives its powers from which Article of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 315 provides for the establishment of a Public Service Commission for each state, including Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: Which amendment to the Constitution of India extended the reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in the Lok Sabha and State Assemblies for another 10 years?
questions.explanation: The 104th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2020 extended the reservation for SCs and STs in Lok Sabha and State Assemblies until 2030.

questions.question: The 'Tamil Nadu Land Reforms (Fixation of Ceiling on Land) Act' aims at:
options: Distributing surplus land to the landless
questions.explanation: The Act fixes a ceiling on agricultural land holdings and provides for the acquisition and distribution of surplus land to landless agricultural labourers.

questions.question: How many tiers are there in the Panchayati Raj system in Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu follows a three-tier Panchayati Raj system: Gram Panchayat, Panchayat Union, and District Panchayat.

questions.question: What is the total number of elected members in the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly has 234 elected members, as per the last delimitation.

questions.question: Which body conducts elections to local bodies in Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: The State Election Commission of Tamil Nadu is responsible for conducting elections to Panchayats and Municipalities.

questions.question: What is the official emblem of the Tamil Nadu government?
questions.explanation: The state emblem of Tamil Nadu features the Gopuram of the Meenakshi Amman Temple.

questions.question: Under which central act does the Tamil Nadu Right to Information framework operate?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu follows the central Right to Information Act, 2005, supplemented by its own rules.

questions.question: Which of the following is a discretionary power of the Governor of Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: The Governor can exercise discretion in appointing the Chief Minister when no party has a clear majority.

questions.question: In which year was the Tamil Nadu Panchayats Act enacted?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Panchayats Act was enacted in 1994 to implement the 73rd Constitutional Amendment.

questions.question: How many seats does Tamil Nadu have in the Rajya Sabha?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu is allocated 18 seats in the Rajya Sabha, the upper house of Parliament.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution is the State Election Commission for Tamil Nadu established?
questions.explanation: Article 243K provides for the establishment of the State Election Commission for local body elections.

questions.question: What is the official language of Tamil Nadu for government business?
questions.explanation: As per the Tamil Nadu Official Language Act, Tamil is the official language, with English permitted for official purposes.

questions.question: Which Article of the Constitution of India empowers the President to appoint a Commission to investigate into the conditions of the socially and educationally backward classes?
questions.explanation: Article 340 allows the President to appoint a Backward Classes Commission to investigate the conditions of socially and educationally backward classes.

questions.question: Under which schedule of the Constitution are the provisions of the Panchayati Raj system found?
questions.explanation: The Eleventh Schedule contains the powers, authority, and responsibilities of Panchayats, added by the 73rd Amendment.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle of State Policy is based on the Gandhian principle of village self-governance?
questions.explanation: Article 40 directs the State to organise village panchayats as units of self-government.

questions.question: The tenure of the Election Commission of India's Chief Election Commissioner is fixed by?
questions.explanation: The Chief Election Commissioner's tenure is not specified in the Constitution; it is determined by the Parliament through the Election Commission (Conditions of Service of Election Commissioners) Act, 1991.

questions.question: In the context of Tamil Nadu, which of the following is a characteristic of the 'Two-Child' norm policy for local body elections?
options: Disqualifies candidates with more than two children
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Local Bodies (Disqualification) Act disqualifies a person from contesting local body elections if they have more than two children.

questions.question: The power to decide the disqualification of a Member of the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly on grounds of defection lies with:
questions.explanation: Under the Tenth Schedule (Anti-Defection Law), the Speaker of the Assembly has the power to decide on disqualification of MLAs for defection.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a Fundamental Right under the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Right to Property was removed as a Fundamental Right by the 44th Amendment (1978) and is now a legal right under Article 300-A.

questions.question: The jurisdiction of the Tamil Nadu State Information Commission extends to:
options: All central government offices in Tamil Nadu
questions.explanation: The State Information Commission has jurisdiction over all public authorities under the state government as per the Right to Information Act, 2005.

questions.question: Under the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act, the Grama Sabha consists of:
options: All registered voters in the village
All adult residents of the village
All government officials in the village
questions.explanation: The Grama Sabha is a body consisting of all persons whose names are included in the electoral rolls for the village panchayat.

questions.question: As per the Tamil Nadu Panchayats Act, 1994, what is the minimum age for a person to be eligible for election as a member of a Panchayat?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment and Tamil Nadu Panchayats Act fix the minimum age for panchayat membership at 21 years.

questions.question: The reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly is provided under which article of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 332 specifically provides for reservation of seats for SCs and STs in the Legislative Assembly of every state.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu State Human Rights Commission consists of a Chairperson and how many members?
questions.explanation: As per the Protection of Human Rights Act, a State Human Rights Commission has a Chairperson and two members.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a power of the Governor of Tamil Nadu?
options: Appointing judges of the Madras High Court
questions.explanation: Judges of the Madras High Court are appointed by the President of India, not the Governor.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu State Information Commission was established in which year?
questions.explanation: Following the Right to Information Act, 2005, the Tamil Nadu State Information Commission was formed in 2006.

questions.question: In which year was the Tamil Nadu Legislative Council abolished?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Legislative Council was abolished by a resolution of the State Assembly passed in 1986.

questions.question: How many members does Tamil Nadu send to the Rajya Sabha?
questions.explanation: As per the Fourth Schedule of the Constitution, Tamil Nadu has 18 seats in the Rajya Sabha.

questions.question: The Governor of Tamil Nadu can reserve a bill for the consideration of the President under which article?
questions.explanation: Article 200 empowers the Governor to reserve certain bills for the President's assent.

questions.question: In the three-tier Panchayati Raj system of Tamil Nadu, which is the intermediate tier?
questions.explanation: The intermediate tier in Tamil Nadu's panchayat system is the Panchayat Union, commonly referred to as Panchayat Samiti.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution empowers the Tamil Nadu Legislature to make laws on matters in the State List?
questions.explanation: Article 246 defines the distribution of legislative powers between the Union and States via the Seventh Schedule.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act, 1994, is based on which constitutional amendment?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) provided constitutional status to Panchayati Raj institutions, which Tamil Nadu adopted via its 1994 Act.

questions.question: The 'Right to Fair Compensation and Transparency in Land Acquisition, Rehabilitation and Resettlement Act, 2013' applies to Tamil Nadu subject to which provision?
questions.explanation: Article 254 deals with inconsistency between Union and State laws; Tamil Nadu's state amendments operate under this provision.

questions.question: How many members are nominated by the Governor to the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly under Article 171?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu has a unicameral legislature (no Legislative Council), so Article 171 (nominated members) does not apply.

questions.question: Which Tamil Nadu government scheme provides free breakfast to students in government primary schools?
questions.explanation: The Chief Minister's Breakfast Scheme was launched in 2022 to provide free breakfast to primary school students.

questions.question: Under the Tamil Nadu Prohibition of Forcible Conversion of Religion Act, 2022, what is the maximum imprisonment for forced conversion of a minor?
questions.explanation: The Act prescribes up to 10 years imprisonment for forced conversion of minors, women, or SC/ST individuals.

questions.question: The 'Tamil Nadu Government Business Rules and Secretariat Instructions' derive their authority from which Article of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 166 deals with the conduct of government business in the state, under which these rules are framed.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution empowers the Parliament to create a High Court for a Union Territory?
questions.explanation: Article 241 allows Parliament to establish a High Court for a Union Territory, such as the Madras High Court's jurisdiction over Puducherry.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act, 1994, provides for a three-tier system. Which level is NOT mandatory in states with a population below 20 lakhs?
questions.explanation: Under the 73rd Amendment, states with population below 20 lakhs may not constitute intermediate (Panchayat Union) or district level panchayats, but Tamil Nadu has all three tiers.

questions.question: In the context of Tamil Nadu, which of the following is a special provision under Article 371-D?
questions.explanation: Article 371-D provides for local cadre and direct recruitment quotas for local candidates in Andhra Pradesh and Telangana, not Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: The 'Tamil Nadu Prohibition of Charging Exorbitant Fees for Education Act' is aimed at regulating which sector?
questions.explanation: This Act regulates fee structures in self-financing professional colleges to prevent capitation fees and ensure affordability.

questions.question: Under the Tamil Nadu State Election Commission, elections to which local body are conducted every five years?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu State Election Commission conducts elections to all three tiers of rural (Panchayats) and urban (Municipalities, Corporations) local bodies.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Directive Principle of State Policy specifically related to the promotion of cottage industries in Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: Article 43 directs the State to promote cottage industries on an individual or cooperative basis in rural areas.

questions.question: The 'Tamil Nadu Government's 'Amma Unavagam' scheme is primarily based on which fundamental right?
questions.explanation: The scheme ensures access to affordable food, which is an extension of the Right to Life under Article 21.

questions.question: Which High Court has the largest number of judges in India?
questions.explanation: The Madras High Court has the highest sanctioned strength of judges (75) among all High Courts in India.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu Legislative Council was abolished in the year?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Legislative Council was abolished by the Tamil Nadu Legislative Council (Abolition) Act, 1986.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of the 'Tamil Nadu Model' of Panchayati Raj?
options: Reservation for women in all tiers
Unified civil service for local bodies
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu provides 33% reservation for women in all Panchayati Raj institutions, a key feature of its model.
